title: Project initialization and scoping
context: Scoped the pokemon_professor game project through dialogue and initialized all project infrastructure including the charter, deliverables scaffold, and Beads issue tracker.

decisions.title: Project named pokemon_professor
decisions.description: The project will be called pokemon_professor throughout all artifacts and tooling.
decisions.rationale: User-specified name chosen at init time.

decisions.title: Browser-based standalone platform
decisions.description: The game will run in a web browser with no server required, built with HTML/CSS/JavaScript.
decisions.rationale: Simplest delivery target for a proof-of-concept game.

decisions.title: Core game concept locked
decisions.description: Student navigates an outdoor university campus and battles 6 professors in a fixed sequence to pass their classes. Each professor is a caricature of their research field.
decisions.rationale: Defined through scoping dialogue; user confirmed the concept before init.

decisions.title: Retro pixel art visual style
decisions.description: All visuals will use simple pixel art in the style of classic 1990s–2000s Pokemon games.
decisions.rationale: User preference; aligns with the Pokemon-inspired format.

decisions.title: Turn-based battle system faithful to Pokemon
decisions.description: Battles are turn-based with moves, attacks, and a run option. The player fights solo. Win = class passed; lose = faint and restart from the beginning.
decisions.rationale: User specified faithfulness to classic Pokemon mechanics.

decisions.title: Proof-of-concept scope target
decisions.description: The initial deliverable is a proof-of-concept with one fully playable end-to-end loop, not a complete game.
decisions.rationale: Keeps scope manageable; user explicitly chose PoC over full prototype.

decisions.title: Explicit scope exclusions
decisions.description: No building interiors, no multiplayer, no save/load, no audio, no catching mechanics.
decisions.rationale: Agreed during scoping to keep the PoC focused.

artifacts_created.summary: Full project charter with overarching objective, purpose, scope commitments, and four validation conditions.
Scaffolded deliverables file with header; no entries yet.

next_session_focus.title: Conceptual grounding
next_session_focus.rationale: background.md is unpopulated; defining professor personas, campus layout, and battle mechanics details will unblock design and implementation work.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is empty; enumerating discrete outputs will clarify what needs to be built for the PoC.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Validation conditions in the charter are a good start but project-specific criteria should be formalised before development begins.

title: Reporting infrastructure fixes
context: Fixed two bugs in the project report: the purpose section now correctly sources from the charter's Overarching Objective, and issues are now loaded from a bd-exported JSONL file rather than a missing static JSON array.

decisions.title: Source report purpose from Overarching Objective
decisions.description: Changed parseCharter() regex from '## Project Purpose' to '## Overarching Objective'.
decisions.rationale: The charter uses 'Overarching Objective' as the top-level section; 'Project Purpose' is a secondary section with different content.

decisions.title: Switch issues loading to JSONL via bd export
decisions.description: Updated loadData() to fetch issues.jsonl and parse with parseJsonl() instead of fetching a JSON array from issues.json.
decisions.rationale: issues.json never existed and would require manual maintenance; bd export --no-memories -o reports/issues.jsonl keeps issue data current with a single command.

artifacts_modified.summary: Fixed charter section pointer and switched issues fetch from JSON to JSONL.

next_session_focus.title: Conceptual grounding
next_session_focus.rationale: background.md is unpopulated; defining professor personas, campus layout, and battle mechanics will unblock design and implementation.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is empty; enumerating discrete outputs clarifies what needs to be built for the PoC.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Formalise project-specific validation conditions before development begins.

title: Scope expansion and artifact schema definition
context: Expanded project scope to include simplicity/readability constraints, dialogue, multi-region maps, music, and weather. Researched vanilla JS game architecture and established the five-module structure and GDD/TDD artifact schemas.

decisions.title: Add readability and simplicity constraints to scope
decisions.description: Flat code structure, inline comments, library vetting required, no build tools. ES modules excluded due to file:// protocol limitation.
decisions.rationale: Owner wants to understand all pieces of the codebase, not just observe it being built.

decisions.title: Expand scope: dialogue, multi-region maps, music, weather
decisions.description: Added dialogue system, building interiors and hidden areas, scene-appropriate background music, and overworld weather animations. Removed prior exclusions for these features.
decisions.rationale: User confirmed all four features are desired for the PoC.

decisions.title: Adopt five-module vanilla JS architecture
decisions.description: data.js, map.js, player.js, battle.js, main.js loaded via script tags. Single central gameState object in main.js. Plain objects for game entities; no class hierarchies.
decisions.rationale: Consistent with real-world projects at this scale; matches readability constraints; no build step required.

decisions.title: Define TDD as per-module files and create GDD as single document
decisions.description: Technical design split into individual tdd/[module].md files. Game design captured in a single game_design.md. Schemas created for both artifact types before authoring begins.
decisions.rationale: Per-module TDD keeps context loading focused; GDD is a single coherent player-facing design document.

artifacts_created.summary: Schema for per-module TDD files covering responsibility, data structures, function contracts, and module interfaces.
Schema for the GDD covering premise, world, professors, battle system, dialogue, audio, and weather.

artifacts_modified.summary: Updated purpose paragraph, expanded in-scope list, removed building/music exclusions from out-of-scope, added VC-05 through VC-09.
Added index entries for technical_design_module and game_design_document schemas.

next_session_focus.title: Author game_design.md — professor personas and campus layout
next_session_focus.rationale: Schemas are defined and scope is settled; conceptual grounding is the immediate blocker for all design and implementation work.

next_session_focus.title: Define project deliverables
next_session_focus.rationale: Deliverables list is still empty; enumerate discrete outputs now that scope is fully defined.

next_session_focus.title: Define success criteria
next_session_focus.rationale: Formalise VC entries against the expanded scope before implementation begins.

title: Game design, technical architecture, and project structure
context: Authored the full GDD and all seven TDD files, finalised the professor lineup and campus map, established the seven-module vanilla JS architecture, added portfolio framing as a project objective, and defined deliverables and success criteria.

decisions.title: Prohibit automated git push during EOS
decisions.description: Removed mandatory git push from Session Completion in CLAUDE.md and added a deny rule for git push in settings.local.json.
decisions.rationale: Pushing to GitHub should be a deliberate, user-initiated action — not an automated step that could fire without review.

decisions.title: Professor lineup and encounter order finalised
decisions.description: Six professors in order: Phonetics (pond), Syntax (classroom), Semantics (library mezzanine), DSA (PhD dungeon), NLP/Deep Learning (GPU lab), Computational Semantics (castle — final boss).
decisions.rationale: Phonetics first sets a warm, sensory tone; Computational Semantics as final boss synthesises all prior subfields and suits the castle setting.

decisions.title: Rendering strategy: Option A — distributed draw functions
decisions.description: Each module exposes a draw(ctx) function; game.js calls them in layer order each frame. No centralised renderer module.
decisions.rationale: Keeps game.js readable as a linear orchestrator — important for the flat, explicit code constraint and for portfolio legibility.

decisions.title: Seven-module architecture with data.js as standalone
decisions.description: Modules: game, engine, data, map, battle, dialogue, audio. data.js is a dedicated static data module; game.js is the entry point and wire-up layer.
decisions.rationale: Separating static data from logic keeps all modules lean and prevents data definitions from being scattered across the codebase.

decisions.title: Portfolio framing added as a project objective
decisions.description: Portfolio Objective section added to project charter. DESIGN.md added as DEL-004. bd remember entry saved.
decisions.rationale: The project serves dual purpose: a playable game and a portfolio artefact demonstrating AI-assisted methodology. Design documents are first-class deliverables.

decisions.title: VC-10 and VC-11 added to success criteria
decisions.description: VC-10 tests the castle gate mechanic (blocked until five professors defeated). VC-11 tests that DESIGN.md is legible to an outside reader.
decisions.rationale: Both scenarios were uncovered by the expanded scope and portfolio objective — neither was covered by VC-01 through VC-09.

artifacts_created.summary: Full GDD: overview, world & setting, all six professors, campus map, battle system, dialogue system, audio, and weather. Placeholders remain for professor names, visual designs, player moves, and some loss dialogue.
TDD for game.js: game loop, scene switching, input routing, module wiring.
TDD for engine.js: gameState schema, all state read/write functions, faint/reset behaviour.
TDD for data.js: all static data schemas (Professor, Move, DialogueSequence, Region, AudioTrack) and exports.
TDD for map.js: tile rendering, player movement, encounter detection, region transitions, weather overlays.
TDD for battle.js: turn loop, move resolution, three effect types, flee mechanic.
TDD for dialogue.js: sequence playback, overlay draw, input suppression.
TDD for audio.js: track management, scene-based switching, no-op guard on redundant calls.

artifacts_modified.summary: Four deliverables defined: playable game (DEL-001), complete GDD (DEL-002), TDD suite (DEL-003), DESIGN.md (DEL-004).
Portfolio Objective section added. VC-10 (castle gate) and VC-11 (DESIGN.md legibility) added to Validation Conditions.
Session Completion section rewritten: git push prohibited during EOS; bd dolt push retained as allowed step.
Deny rule added for Bash(git push*) to block automated pushes at the tool level.

resolved_issues.title: Conceptual grounding
resolved_issues.resolution: game_design.md authored with all six professors, campus map, battle system, dialogue system, audio, and weather sections complete.

resolved_issues.title: Define project deliverables
resolved_issues.resolution: Four deliverables defined in project_context/deliverables.md with linked Beads issues.

resolved_issues.title: Define success criteria
resolved_issues.resolution: VC-01 through VC-11 confirmed in project_charter.md; added VC-10 and VC-11 for castle gate and portfolio documentation.

resolved_issues.title: Author TDD files for all seven modules
resolved_issues.resolution: All seven TDD files written in tdd/.

next_session_focus.title: Name all six professors
next_session_focus.rationale: Professor names are the most visible placeholder in the GDD and unblock visual design, dialogue finalisation, and data.js authoring.

next_session_focus.title: Define player move list
next_session_focus.rationale: Player moves are needed to complete the battle system design and are a prereq for finalising the GDD and starting implementation.

next_session_focus.title: Begin game implementation
next_session_focus.rationale: All architecture decisions are settled and TDDs are written — implementation can start once GDD placeholders are resolved or in parallel with lower-priority ones.

title: Professor naming, player moves, and GDD restructure
context: Finalised the full professor roster with punny portmanteau names, expanded the player move list to six moves, and split the monolithic GDD into a gdd/ folder mirroring the tdd/ structure.

decisions.title: Professor roster finalised — 7 professors
decisions.description: Schwaartz (Phonetics), Syntaxa (Syntax), Composita (Semantics), Recursio (DSA), Bayesio (NLP), Vec Tor (Comp. Semantics / final boss), Parsemore (reserve).
decisions.rationale: Punny portmanteau naming convention adopted from user proposal. Seven professors designed with six in active use; Parsemore interchangeable with Recursio at position 4.

decisions.title: Vec Tor and Bayesio field assignments swapped
decisions.description: Vec Tor moved to Comp. Semantics final boss slot; Bayesio moved to NLP slot.
decisions.rationale: Vector semantics is a legitimate computational semantics framework; Bayesian methods are core to NLP. The swap improves thematic coherence for both professors.

decisions.title: Professor move count changed from 3 to 4
decisions.description: Each professor now has four moves. New moves added to Schwaartz (Aspiration), Composita (Deixis), Recursio (full set redesigned). Parsemore, Bayesio, Syntaxa, and Vec Tor move sets defined by user.
decisions.rationale: User proposed 4-move sets for the named professors; count standardised across the roster.

decisions.title: Player move list expanded to 6
decisions.description: Counterexample (22 dmg), Cite This! (15 dmg + skip), All-Nighter (38 dmg, 10 self), Hot Take (10 dmg, ignores debuffs), Non-Sequitur (0 dmg, halves professor next move), Correction (20 dmg, doubles to 40 if professor last move ≥30).
decisions.rationale: Six moves provide tactical variety: reliable damage, high-cost finisher, disruption, reactive counter, status clear, and soft debuff.

decisions.title: GDD split into gdd/ folder
decisions.description: game_design.md replaced with an index; content distributed across gdd/overview.md, player.md, professors.md, map.md, battle.md, dialogue.md, audio.md.
decisions.rationale: File was becoming unwieldy. Structure mirrors the tdd/ folder for consistency.

artifacts_created.summary: Premise, tone, world and setting
Player character stats and full 6-move player move list
Full roster of 7 professors with names, personalities, moves, HP, and dialogue
Campus regions, key locations, visual style, weather and atmosphere
Battle system rules
Dialogue system
Audio tracks

artifacts_modified.summary: Replaced with a thin index pointing to gdd/ section files

resolved_issues.title: Name all six professors
resolved_issues.resolution: Seven professors named and fully specified in gdd/professors.md

resolved_issues.title: Define player move list
resolved_issues.resolution: Six player moves defined with damage values and effects in gdd/player.md

next_session_focus.title: Finalise GDD — resolve remaining placeholders
next_session_focus.rationale: Visual design notes and post-battle loss dialogue are still marked placeholder. Resolving these unblocks data.js authoring and completes the GDD as a deliverable.

next_session_focus.title: Begin game implementation
next_session_focus.rationale: Architecture is settled, TDDs written, professor roster and moves locked. Implementation can start; data.js is a natural first file since it has no dependencies.

next_session_focus.title: Write DESIGN.md
next_session_focus.rationale: Portfolio framing is an explicit project objective. DESIGN.md explains the methodology to reviewers and is independent of implementation progress.

title: GDD finalisation, visual asset pipeline, and per-professor battle music
context: Resolved all remaining GDD placeholders by writing visual design notes for all seven professors — iterating through three approaches before settling on grounded-eccentric designs with one distinctive physical feature per character. Defined the full visual asset production pipeline and updated the audio spec to use per-professor battle tracks.

decisions.title: Professor visual designs finalised
decisions.description: Each professor has a written visual design note: realistic outfit, one memorable physical feature (e.g. Syntaxa — extraordinarily bushy eyebrows; Vec Tor — long patrician nose), stern gym-leader expression. Gender assigned: Schwaartz, Composita, Bayesio, Parsemore (F); Syntaxa, Recursio, Vec Tor (M).
decisions.rationale: Earlier attempts (realistic then absurdist) were either too bland or too complex for AI image generation. The final approach balances memorability with prompt-generatability.

decisions.title: Visual asset pipeline defined
decisions.description: Battle sprites: AI-generated via Midjourney at 96x96px, Schwaartz used as style anchor via --sref. Overworld sprites: hand-drawn in Aseprite at 24x24px. Sprite sheet layouts specified. Files live in assets/sprites/battle/ and assets/sprites/overworld/.
decisions.rationale: Two-tier system mirrors the Pokemon source material. Battle sprites need detail; overworld sprites need only silhouette and colour at that resolution. AI generation is unsuitable at 24x24.

decisions.title: Per-professor battle music adopted
decisions.description: Each professor has their own battle track instead of a single shared theme. Track ids follow the pattern battle_<professorId>. game.js calls audio.switchTo with the professor id on battle entry.
decisions.rationale: User sourced individual tracks for all seven professors. Per-professor music increases variety and reinforces each professor character.

artifacts_created.summary: Full visual asset production spec: two-tier sprite system, asset inventory, sprite sheet layouts, file naming conventions, production workflow, and data.js reference format.

artifacts_modified.summary: Visual design notes written for all 7 professors across three iterative drafts.
Single battle theme row replaced with 7 per-professor battle track entries.
tracks object updated to include dungeon/indoor/cafeteria area tracks and all 7 per-professor battle tracks; switchTo call pattern documented in module interfaces.

resolved_issues.title: Define professor visual designs
resolved_issues.resolution: Visual design notes written for all 7 professors.

resolved_issues.title: Write post-battle loss dialogue for all professors
resolved_issues.resolution: Confirmed already complete from prior session GDD restructure; no additional work required.

resolved_issues.title: Finalise game_design.md — resolve all placeholders
resolved_issues.resolution: All placeholders resolved; GDD is complete as a deliverable.

next_session_focus.title: Begin game implementation
next_session_focus.rationale: GDD is now complete. Architecture settled, TDDs written, roster and moves locked. data.js is the natural first file — no dependencies, unblocks everything else.

next_session_focus.title: Write DESIGN.md
next_session_focus.rationale: Portfolio framing is an explicit project objective and is independent of implementation progress.

next_session_focus.title: Source victory and defeat jingles
next_session_focus.rationale: User confirmed these are still planned. Required before the audio module is fully testable.

title: data.js implementation, browser test runner, and coding brief pattern
context: Implemented js/data.js as the complete static data layer for the game, translating all GDD content (professors, moves, dialogue, regions, audio) into importable JS. Established a browser-based test runner with 35 passing tests and introduced a coding brief pattern to reduce context load for future implementation sessions.

decisions.title: data.js implemented as first module
decisions.description: All static game data written to js/data.js as ES module exports. Tile maps are 10x8 placeholder stubs pending full map design.
decisions.rationale: No dependencies; unblocks all other modules. Natural starting point confirmed by prior session planning.

decisions.title: Browser-based test runner adopted
decisions.description: Minimal tests/runner.js with test() and assert helpers; tests/data.test.js with 35 tests; served via python3 -m http.server. No Node.js required.
decisions.rationale: No JS runtime installed; approach is consistent with the no-build-step project constraint and equally legible for portfolio reviewers.

decisions.title: professorMoves added as a named export
decisions.description: data TDD omitted professorMoves from its exports table, but battle.js requires it to resolve professor move ids to damage values. Added and flagged with a comment.
decisions.rationale: Without it battle.js has no path to professor move data. TDD gap tracked as test_project-xw5.

decisions.title: Five additional player move effect strings introduced
decisions.description: skip, player_recoil, clear_debuff, halve_next, counter — beyond the three in the battle TDD. Defined in data.js with comments noting battle.js must implement them.
decisions.rationale: Player moves require distinct mechanics not covered by the existing three effect strings. Tracked as test_project-xw5.

decisions.title: Coding brief pattern established
decisions.description: briefs/ directory created. briefs/engine.md written as the first example — compact doc extracting the relevant data.js values inline and compressing TDD function specs to a table.
decisions.rationale: Replaces loading both a full TDD and data.js when implementing a module. Reduces context overhead for implementation sessions.

artifacts_created.summary: Complete static data layer: 6 professors, 24 professor moves, 6 player moves, all dialogue sequences, 7 regions with placeholder tile maps, 15 audio tracks.
Minimal browser test runner with test(), assert helpers, and renderResults().
35-test suite for data.js covering structural completeness, referential integrity, value constraints, and cross-checks. All passing.
Test entry point — imports runner and test files, calls renderResults().
Coding brief for engine.js: concrete data.js values extracted inline, function specs in table form. Template for remaining module briefs.

resolved_issues.title: Define testing strategy
resolved_issues.resolution: Browser-based runner implemented (tests/runner.js). 35 tests written for data.js, all passing. Strategy: no build step, no dependencies, served via python3 -m http.server.

next_session_focus.title: Write coding briefs for remaining modules
next_session_focus.rationale: Briefs for map, battle, dialogue, audio, and game should be written before implementing each module. engine.md is the template. Do this first to keep implementation sessions lean.

next_session_focus.title: Implement engine.js
next_session_focus.rationale: engine.js has no dependencies and is required by every other module. Brief already written. Natural next implementation step.

next_session_focus.title: Create battle sprites for Bayesio and Vec Tor
next_session_focus.rationale: Two professor sprites missing from assets/sprites/battle/. Can proceed in parallel with implementation but needed before the game is visually complete.

title: Phaser 3 migration — TDD rewrites and battle scene milestone scoping
context: Pivoted the entire implementation from vanilla JS canvas rendering to Phaser 3. All six rendering/orchestration TDDs were rewritten to describe Phaser Scene architecture, the project charter was updated with a formal library vetting decision, and the first implementation milestone was scoped to a playable BattleScene against Prof. Schwaartz.

decisions.title: Switch from vanilla JS to Phaser 3
decisions.description: All game rendering, scene management, input, audio, and the game loop will use Phaser 3, loaded as a local vendor file.
decisions.rationale: Eliminates five categories of manual implementation; reduces complexity for a non-specialist reader given that the Phaser Scene lifecycle is well-documented and will be explained once in DESIGN.md. Preserves the no-build-step constraint.

decisions.title: Overworld sprite size changed from 24×24 to 32×32 px; tileset tile size changed from 24×24 to 16×16 px
decisions.description: Overworld character sprites are now specified at 32×32 px; tileset tiles at 16×16 px.
decisions.rationale: Most available tilesets are 16×16. The 32×32/16×16 ratio is the classic Pokemon character-to-tile proportion and significantly expands the pool of usable off-the-shelf tileset assets.

decisions.title: Tilemap implementation uses inline 2D arrays from data.js
decisions.description: OverworldScene will use this.make.tilemap({ data: region.tileMap, tileWidth: 16, tileHeight: 16 }) to build tilemaps directly from data.js arrays.
decisions.rationale: data.js already stores tile maps as 2D arrays. Phaser supports this natively, avoiding the need for external Tiled JSON files.

decisions.title: AudioScene adopted as persistent Phaser scene
decisions.description: Audio is managed by a dedicated AudioScene that runs continuously and is never stopped, ensuring music persists across scene transitions.
decisions.rationale: Phaser Sound Manager is scene-scoped; a persistent scene is the cleanest way to maintain continuous background music across overworld/battle transitions.

decisions.title: First implementation milestone scoped to BattleScene with Schwaartz
decisions.description: The first playable target is a BattleScene that boots directly, loads Schwaartz assets, and runs a complete battle with music. Prerequisite issues identified and dependency chain wired.
decisions.rationale: Battle is the most self-contained scene; it depends only on engine.js and AudioScene, not on the overworld, tilemap, or dialogue systems.

artifacts_modified.summary: Rewritten for Phaser.Game bootstrap — main.js replaces the vanilla game loop
Rewritten as OverworldScene; tilemap approach corrected to use inline 2D arrays from data.js
Rewritten as BattleScene with Phaser Scene lifecycle and GameObjects
Rewritten as DialogueScene launched as a persistent overlay
Rewritten as AudioScene; track keys corrected to match data.js (battle_prof_schwaartz pattern)
Module Interfaces updated: all modules changed to all Phaser scenes
professorMoves added to exports table; Module Interfaces updated to Phaser scene names; init() description updated
Asset Loading section updated for Phaser load pattern; overworld sprite dimensions updated to 32×32 px; tileset tile size updated to 16×16 px
External library section updated with formal Phaser 3 vetting decision
DEL-003 note updated to flag TDD revision via test_project-csz

resolved_issues.title: Rewrite TDDs and charter for Phaser 3 migration
resolved_issues.resolution: All six rendering/orchestration TDDs rewritten; charter and deliverables updated; data.md and engine.md patched

next_session_focus.title: Download Phaser 3 and implement engine.js
next_session_focus.rationale: Both are unblocked and are prerequisites for the entire implementation chain. Download Phaser first (test_project-urs), then implement engine.js (test_project-2kh) — together they unblock index.html, AudioScene, and BattleScene.

next_session_focus.title: Implement BattleScene milestone
next_session_focus.rationale: Once Phaser, engine.js, AudioScene, and the player sprite move are complete, implement BattleScene.js for a playable battle against Schwaartz with music.

next_session_focus.title: Move player battle sprite to assets/sprites/battle/
next_session_focus.rationale: Quick prerequisite — move battle_alt/player.png to battle/player.png so BattleScene can load it from the correct path.

title: Battle milestone — engine, BattleScene, AudioScene, and game bootstrap
context: Implemented the full battle milestone: downloaded Phaser 3, wrote engine.js, BattleScene.js, AudioScene.js, main.js, and index.html. Fixed sprite size spec and overworld size inconsistencies in the visuals TDD. The game now opens to a playable Schwaartz battle with music via a local server.

decisions.title: Battle sprite size updated to 192×192 px
decisions.description: tdd/visuals.md updated from 96×96 to 192×192 throughout (two-tier table, sprite sheet section, production workflow, constraints).
decisions.rationale: Existing sprites are 192×192; downscaling to 96×96 would degrade pixel art quality. Consistency is the key constraint, and all current sprites are at the same resolution.

decisions.title: Overworld sprite size corrected to 32×32 in TDD
decisions.description: Production workflow and constraints sections of tdd/visuals.md still referenced 24×24 from before last session's decision. Updated to 32×32 (frame size), 64×32 (professor sheet), 96×128 (player sheet).
decisions.rationale: Leftover inconsistency from Session 8's 24×24 → 32×32 decision. The two-tier table was already correct; only the workflow and constraints sections were stale.

decisions.title: All player move effects implemented in BattleScene
decisions.description: data.js defines 5 player-side move effects (skip, player_recoil, clear_debuff, halve_next, counter) not documented in tdd/battle.md. All are handled in BattleScene.resolveTurn().
decisions.rationale: data.js comment explicitly describes these effects and they are assigned to player moves. Implementing them gives correct battle behaviour; omitting them would leave moves behaving as null-effect moves.

decisions.title: OverworldScene and DialogueScene stubbed for milestone
decisions.description: Minimal stub scenes created so the battle flow (OverworldScene → BattleScene → DialogueScene → OverworldScene) completes end-to-end. The stub OverworldScene launches AudioScene and BattleScene with Schwaartz; the stub DialogueScene calls onComplete immediately.
decisions.rationale: Issue test_project-3cy explicitly allowed stubs for the battle milestone. Full implementations are in the backlog.

artifacts_created.summary: Phaser 3 v3.90.0 minified build, loaded locally with no CDN dependency
Records library version, source URL, and addition policy
Game state module — all 10 exports per TDD: init, getState, setPlayerHP, setPlayerPosition, setRegion, setScene, setPendingEncounter, defeatProfessor, isDefeated, allProfessorsDefeated, resetGame

Persistent audio manager: loads all tracks from data.audioTracks, exposes switchTo, play, stop
Milestone stub: launches AudioScene and BattleScene (Schwaartz), sleeps self as wake target
Milestone stub: calls onComplete immediately and stops self
Phaser.Game entry point: 480×320 config, all 4 scenes registered, arcade physics
Game entry point: loads vendor/phaser.min.js locally, imports js/main.js as ES module

artifacts_modified.summary: Battle sprite size updated 96×96 → 192×192 (4 occurrences); overworld sprite size corrected 24×24 → 32×32 in production workflow and constraints (3 occurrences)

next_session_focus.rationale: Schwaartz will render as a Phaser missing-texture placeholder until data.js is corrected. Quick fix — either rename the file or update the sprite path.

title: Battle polish — animated HP bars, multi-level sprites, engine tests, music fix
context: Extended the battle milestone with animated HP bar drains, multi-level professor sprites that unlock as HP decreases, and a sequenced turn display. Fixed three bugs discovered during testing: engine.init() never called (player HP bar unrendered), loss detection broken by engine's faint-reset, and battle music blocked by browser autoplay policy and AudioScene race condition.

decisions.title: Multi-level professor sprites via sprites[] array
decisions.description: Added a sprites[] field to professor data entries (initially Schwaartz). BattleScene loads all three textures at preload and swaps them via _updateProfSprite() as HP crosses 66% and 33% thresholds.
decisions.rationale: Gives professors visual personality that escalates with the battle; sprites already existed as _l1/_l2/_l3 files so no new assets were needed. Also resolved the existing sprite path mismatch bug.

decisions.title: HP bar drain animations via Phaser tweens
decisions.description: Replaced the flat lines[] + LOG_DISPLAY_MS approach with a _runSequence() runner that chains text steps and HP animation steps. Each step is a closure; _animateProfHP and _animatePlayerHP use tweens.addCounter to tween displayed HP each frame.
decisions.rationale: Matches the user's requested sequence: attack text → bar drains → professor attacks → bar drains. Separating draw helpers (_drawProfHPBar, _drawPlayerHPBar) from drawHPBars() was necessary to allow per-frame updates during tweens.

decisions.title: engine.init() called in OverworldScene.create()
decisions.description: engine.js exports init() but it was never called. gameState started as {}, making playerHP undefined and causing all HP bar draw calls to produce NaN.
decisions.rationale: OverworldScene is the game entry point; calling init() there ensures state is valid before any scene reads it.

decisions.title: Loss detection fixed to use raw HP value
decisions.description: engine.setPlayerHP(n) internally calls resetGame() when n<=0, restoring HP to 100. Checking engine.getState().playerHP<=0 after the call was always false. Fixed by checking the raw pre-clamp value (toHP<=0) in resolveTurn.
decisions.rationale: Side effect of the engine design; the animation refactor exposed it because HP values were being captured before each change anyway.

decisions.title: Music deferred to sound 'unlocked' event
decisions.description: BattleScene.create() now checks this.sound.locked and defers switchTo() via sound.once('unlocked',...) if true.
decisions.rationale: Browsers block audio until a user gesture. AudioScene is also loaded in parallel and may not have its tracks map populated when BattleScene.create() runs. Both issues are resolved by waiting for the unlock event, which fires on first keypress.

decisions.title: White battle background
decisions.description: Background, panel, and border colours changed to white/light grey. All text and HP bar track colours updated to dark tones for readability.
decisions.rationale: User preference.

artifacts_created.summary: 18 tests covering engine.init(), HP clamping, loss state (faint/respawn), win state (defeatProfessor idempotency), and allProfessorsDefeated() logic.

artifacts_modified.summary: Added sprites[] array to Schwaartz with three level paths; updated sprite field to point to _l1; extended comment block to document the multi-level convention.

Major refactor of resolveTurn() to build a visual sequence (seq[]) instead of a flat lines array. Added _runSequence, _showLine, _animateProfHP, _animatePlayerHP, _drawProfHPBar, _drawPlayerHPBar, _endSeq, _updateProfSprite. Deferred music start. White background. Fixed loss detection.
Added engine import and engine.init() call at top of create().
Added 4 tests for the new sprites[] shape: length==3, non-empty paths, sprites[0] matches sprite field, Schwaartz paths point to correct _l1/_l2/_l3 files.
Added import for engine.test.js.

resolved_issues.title: Fix schwaartz sprite path mismatch in data.js
resolved_issues.resolution: Resolved as part of multi-level sprite implementation — sprite field now points to schwaartz_l1.png and sprites[] covers all three levels.

next_session_focus.title: Manual dialogue advance in battle
next_session_focus.rationale: Currently text lines auto-advance on a timer. Changing to button-press advance (Space/Enter) is a prerequisite for the hit animations issue, since the prompt indicator and input gating need to be in place before layering sound and animation on top.

next_session_focus.title: Hit animations and sound effects
next_session_focus.rationale: Prof sprite flash and screen jitter will make the battle feel substantially more alive. Depends on dialogue advance being in place first. SFX assets still need to be sourced.

next_session_focus.title: Write coding briefs for remaining modules
next_session_focus.rationale: OverworldScene, DialogueScene, and MapScene are still stubs. Writing briefs while the battle scene is fresh keeps design decisions consistent across scenes.

title: Manual dialogue advance, hit animations, SFX wiring
context: Implemented player-controlled dialogue advance in the battle scene (Space/Enter replaces auto-timer) and layered in hit animations — professor sprite flash, camera shake on player hit — plus SFX wiring with 2-tier intensity tiering. All battle audio tracks registered in data.js with placeholder paths pending asset sourcing.

decisions.title: Manual dialogue advance via _advanceCallback pattern
decisions.description: _showLine() stores the next callback in this._advanceCallback; the confirmMove handler checks for it first and invokes it on Space/Enter, hiding the ▼ prompt.
decisions.rationale: Gives the player full control over reading pace; prerequisite for hit animations as input gating must be in place before layering effects on top.

decisions.title: SFX implemented with placeholder paths (option A)
decisions.description: Three SFX entries added to audioTracks in data.js with paths assets/sfx/hit_light.mp3, hit_heavy.mp3, dialogue_adv.mp3. AudioScene.play() silently no-ops for missing files.
decisions.rationale: Avoids blocking code progress on asset sourcing; the wiring is complete and files can be dropped in without any code changes.

decisions.title: Hit animations concurrent with HP drain
decisions.description: _flashProfSprite() and cameras.main.shake() are fire-and-forget, called at the start of _animateProfHP / _animatePlayerHP when from > to. Both finish before the 800ms HP tween completes.
decisions.rationale: Concurrent execution gives immediate visual feedback at the moment of impact without extending the total sequence duration.

decisions.title: BattleScene test gap documented as P4 backlog
decisions.description: No unit tests added for BattleScene.js changes. Filed test_project-8e1 to document the gap.
decisions.rationale: Phaser scenes require a live browser + WebGL context; the project's current test runner cannot exercise them. Addressing this would require browser automation (Playwright) which is out of scope for the proof-of-concept target.

artifacts_created.summary: Directory placeholder for battle SFX files not yet sourced

artifacts_modified.summary: Manual dialogue advance, _advanceCallback pattern, ▼ prompt, flash/shake animations, _playHitSfx helper, LINE_DISPLAY_MS removed
Three SFX audioTrack entries added
Two new tests for SFX audioTrack presence and loop values; if(track) guard replaced with explicit assertion

resolved_issues.title: Battle text: advance dialogue via button press instead of auto-timer
resolved_issues.resolution: Implemented via _advanceCallback pattern in BattleScene.js

resolved_issues.title: Battle hit animations and hit sound effects
resolved_issues.resolution: Professor sprite flash, camera shake on player hit, and 2-tier SFX wiring implemented

next_session_focus.rationale: Three audio files (hit_light.mp3, hit_heavy.mp3, dialogue_adv.mp3) needed in assets/sfx/ before battle sounds play. Code is fully wired — drop files in, no code changes needed.

next_session_focus.title: Create battle sprites for Prof. Bayesio and Prof. Vec Tor
next_session_focus.rationale: Only Schwaartz has multi-level sprites; the remaining professors use a single static image. Adding l1/l2/l3 sprites for at least one more professor would make the sprite-level system feel earned.

next_session_focus.title: Write coding briefs for remaining modules
next_session_focus.rationale: OverworldScene, DialogueScene, and MapScene are still stubs. Briefs now while the battle scene is complete keeps design decisions consistent across scenes.

title: Battle UI overhaul: square canvas, two-level menu, move descriptions
context: Resized the game canvas to 400×400 for a more square aspect ratio and refitted all BattleScene layout constants. Introduced a two-level battle menu (Fight/Run/Item → move submenu) and filtered the player moveset to four active moves. Added a dynamic move description panel in the right column of the UI panel. Filed a P1 Beads issue for a BattleScene test suite using a Phaser mock strategy.

decisions.title: Canvas resized to 400×400
decisions.description: Changed game config width/height from 480×320 to 400×400 and refitted all BattleScene layout constants proportionally.
decisions.rationale: Player could not see the full move set; more vertical space in the UI panel and less wasted horizontal space improves legibility.

decisions.title: Two-level battle menu
decisions.description: Added a top-level action menu (Fight / Run / Item) before the move submenu. ESC in the move submenu returns to the action menu rather than fleeing. Post-turn resolution always returns to the action menu.
decisions.rationale: Classic Pokemon-style UI hierarchy makes the battle feel more intentional and leaves room for future Item/Run expansions.

decisions.title: Player moveset filtered to four active moves
decisions.description: ACTIVE_MOVE_IDS constant in BattleScene selects [non_sequitur, all_nighter, counterexample, correction] from the six moves in data.js.
decisions.rationale: User specified the four moves to use; filtering in BattleScene keeps data.js and its tests intact.

decisions.title: Move description panel
decisions.description: Added moveDescText in the right column of the UI panel, updated by _updateMoveDesc() on every cursor movement in the move submenu.
decisions.rationale: Gives the player context for each move without leaving the battle screen.

decisions.title: BattleScene tests filed as P1 with Phaser mock strategy
decisions.description: Created test_project-1d6 to track writing tests/battle.test.js and tests/mocks/phaser.mock.js. Agreed on Option A: stub Phaser.Scene and all game object methods so BattleScene is importable in the test harness.
decisions.rationale: Portfolio objective requires disciplined, documented methodology; untested battle logic is the highest-priority gap.

artifacts_modified.summary: Canvas dimensions changed to 400×400.
Layout constants refitted; two-level menu, ACTIVE_MOVE_IDS filter, moveDescText description panel added.

next_session_focus.title: Write BattleScene unit tests with Phaser mock
next_session_focus.rationale: P1 and highest-priority gap: agreed on Phaser mock strategy (Option A), deliverables scoped, ready to implement.

next_session_focus.title: Source battle SFX files
next_session_focus.rationale: Code fully wired — drop hit_light.mp3, hit_heavy.mp3, dialogue_adv.mp3 into assets/sfx/ and sounds play immediately.

next_session_focus.title: Write coding briefs for remaining modules
next_session_focus.rationale: OverworldScene, DialogueScene, MapScene still stubs; brief while battle decisions are fresh.

title: BattleScene refactor, sprite integration, debug tooling
context: Refactored BattleScene.js with two targeted improvements: a centralised _setUIMode helper and a split resolveTurn into three sub-functions with a ctx object. Integrated three-level sprites for all professors, fixed pixel art rendering, and built a standalone battle debug selector page.

decisions.title: _setUIMode centralises UI visibility
decisions.description: Added _setUIMode('action'|'moves'|'log') replacing 5 scattered setVisible blocks across confirmMove, backOrFlee, selectMove, resolveTurn callback, and _showNoItems.
decisions.rationale: Removes repetition and makes UI state transitions auditable from one place without adding architectural complexity.

decisions.title: resolveTurn split into sub-functions with ctx object
decisions.description: Extracted _applyDeferredDamage(ctx), _applyPlayerMove(ctx), _applyProfessorTurn(ctx); resolveTurn becomes a thin orchestrator. Effect switch statements kept inline rather than converted to handler maps.
decisions.rationale: Reduces resolveTurn from ~150 lines to ~30; each phase is now independently readable. Handler maps rejected — charter mandates flat, explicit code readable without architectural knowledge.

decisions.title: pixelArt: true in Phaser game config
decisions.description: Added pixelArt: true to main.js Phaser config, enabling nearest-neighbour texture filtering and roundPixels.
decisions.rationale: Eliminates dark fringe artifact caused by WebGL linear filtering interpolating opaque edge pixels against transparent (black) pixels when sprites are scaled.

decisions.title: All professors upgraded to three-level sprites
decisions.description: Syntaxa, Composita, Recursio, Bayesio, Vec Tor given sprites[] arrays in data.js with l1/l2/l3 paths; sprite field updated to l1 fallback. TODO comments removed.
decisions.rationale: All battle PNGs now available; dynamic sprite-swap on HP thresholds now active for all six professors.

decisions.title: debug.html + DebugSelectorScene for battle testing
decisions.description: Created debug.html and DebugSelectorScene (registered as 'OverworldScene') providing a click-to-select battle debug page. After each battle BattleScene wakes the selector automatically.
decisions.rationale: Option B chosen over URL params for better UX when cycling through all six professor battles. No modifications to BattleScene required — selector key matches what endBattle() expects.

artifacts_created.summary: Debug battle selector scene registered as OverworldScene; shows all professors with HP; relaunches on battle end
Standalone Phaser debug page booting DebugSelectorScene, BattleScene, DialogueScene, AudioScene
26 browser-based unit tests for _applyDeferredDamage, _applyPlayerMove, _applyProfessorTurn, _setUIMode
Minimal Phaser 3 global stub enabling BattleScene import in non-Phaser test environment

artifacts_modified.summary: Added _setUIMode, _applyDeferredDamage, _applyPlayerMove, _applyProfessorTurn; resolveTurn slimmed to orchestrator
Added pixelArt: true to Phaser game config
Five professors upgraded from single sprite to sprites[] three-level arrays; TODO comments removed
Added battle_scene.test.js import

title: Dev hub, architecture review, Parsemore secret boss
context: Replaced index.html with a dev hub giving three launch options (Full Game, Battle Debug, Run Tests). Ran a full architecture review; fixed two issues in BattleScene.js. Added Prof. Parsemore as a fully wired secret boss with a code-gated courtyard location.

decisions.title: index.html → dev hub; game entry point moved to game.html
decisions.description: index.html now presents three launch options rather than starting the game directly. The game runs from game.html.
decisions.rationale: Player was landing in test mode with a 404. A named dev hub makes the launch intent explicit and prevents accidental test-mode entry.

decisions.title: BattleScene reads prof.battleMusic instead of deriving track ID
decisions.description: Changed 'battle_' + this.professorId to prof.battleMusic in BattleScene.js:308.
decisions.rationale: Architecture review finding: the data contract field was being ignored in favour of a string-concatenation convention, creating a fragile implicit dependency.

decisions.title: ACTIVE_MOVE_IDS annotated to explain 4-of-6 selection
decisions.description: Added a comment to BattleScene.js explaining that cite_this and hot_take are intentionally excluded from the active battle UI for this milestone.
decisions.rationale: Architecture review finding: undocumented divergence between data.js (6 moves) and BattleScene (4 active).

decisions.title: Prof. Parsemore added as secret boss
decisions.description: 200 HP, courtyard region (code-gated via graduate lounge north exit), 4 new moves (chunker, dep_parse/disrupt, corpus_crush/self_damage, full_parse/deferred), full dialogue, sprites and music already present. engine.js PRE_CASTLE check updated to slice(0,-2).
decisions.rationale: User decision: Parsemore promoted from reserve to secret post-game boss, accessible via a hidden courtyard requiring a code.

artifacts_created.summary: Game entry point — former index.html content, starts OverworldScene via main.js

artifacts_modified.summary: Replaced with dev hub: three links to Full Game, Battle Debug, and Run Tests
Architecture fixes: prof.battleMusic field used for track ID; ACTIVE_MOVE_IDS comment added; player sprite scale 0.5→0.55
Added Parsemore moves (chunker, dep_parse, corpus_crush, full_parse), professor entry (200 HP, courtyard), dialogue sequences, courtyard region, and graduate_lounge north exit connection
PRE_CASTLE_PROFESSOR_IDS updated to slice(0,-2) to exclude both vec_tor and Parsemore
Professor count assertion updated from 6 to 7

next_session_focus.title: Write DESIGN.md — methodology overview for portfolio
next_session_focus.rationale: High-value portfolio deliverable; battle system is stable and Parsemore's addition gives a good example of iterative design decisions to showcase.
